zh.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice於1377年出生在愛爾蘭迪 本 克里。她於1396年與Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克費爾莫易結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在愛爾蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice于1377年出生在爱尔兰迪 本 克里。她于1396年与Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche在爱尔兰庫迪 科克費爾莫易结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在爱尔兰。

nameMeanings.name: FitzMaurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

en.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice was born in 1377, in Diocese of Kerry, Ireland. She married Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche in 1396, in Fermoy, County Cork, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice est née en 1377, à Diocese of Kerry, Irlande. Elle a épousé Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche en 1396, à Fermoy, County Cork, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice wurde 1377 in Diocese of Kerry, Irland geboren. Sie hat 1396 in Fermoy, County Cork, Irland Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice è nata il nel 1377 nel Diocese of Kerry, Irlanda. Lei sposato Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche nel 1396, nel Fermoy, County Cork, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMauriceは1377年にアイルランドディオセゼ オフ ケリーで生まれた。1396年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクフエルモイでMaurice FitzJohn de la Rocheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はアイルランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice(은)는 1377년 아일랜드 디오스스 프 케리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1396년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 퍼모이에서 Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 아일랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice nasceu em 1377, em Diocese of Kerry, Irlanda. Ela casou-se com Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche em 1396, em Fermoy, County Cork, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice родилась в 1377 г. в местности диосес оф кирри, Ирландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche в 1396 г. в местности фирмои, коунти корк, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Amy FitzMaurice nació en 1377, en Diocese of Kerry, Irlanda. Se casó con Maurice FitzJohn de la Roche en 1396, en Fermoy, County Cork, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en Irlanda.
